Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for all ExpenseBot remediation tickets with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "expense" or "auto-categorizer" (or similar) using linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue to find the relevant tickets on project proj_expense_autocategorizer: ENG-211 (pilot monitoring, assigned to Samira, in progress), ENG-212 (edge case coverage, assigned to Dmitri, in progress, due 4/24), ENG-213 (bulk export, assigned to Anh, backlog), ENG-214 (Vectral config fix, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 4/22), ENG-215 (Mosaic config fix, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 4/22), ENG-216 (audit of 47 expenses, assigned to Dmitri, in progress, due 4/23, P0), ENG-217 (pipeline hardening, assigned to Lena, in progress, due 4/24), ENG-218 (dashboard type validation, assigned to Anh, in progress, due 4/25), ENG-219 (launch readiness checklist, assigned to Samira, to do, due 4/25), ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection, assigned to Dmitri, to do, due 4/29).
OE 2: Read Linear issue comments with a query related to each remediation ticket using linear_list_comments or linear_get_issue to get detailed status updates on each ticket. The agent needs to discover deployment verifications, completion signals, and blockers from the comments on each ticket.
OE 3: Search Slack #engineering channel (C003) with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "pipeline" or "dashboard" or "remediation" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history to find: (a) Anh Nguyen's message reporting that after Lena's pipeline hardening deploy (ENG-217), the admin dashboard policy editor is throwing 500 errors when loading the Mosaic config, blocking his work on ENG-218; (b) Lena Bjorkstrom's reply acknowledging the regression and saying she'll add a migration step to fix it by Monday; (c) existing ExpenseBot discussions including Dmitri's comprehensive audit findings and Samira's remediation plan.
OE 4: Search emails with a query related to "Dmitri" or "receipt" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate" (or similar) using search_emails to find TWO emails: (a) Dmitri's original audit findings email (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings) documenting 3 suspicious TechComfort Supply receipts totaling $223, Kevin Tran $89 (Vectral, paid), Andre Washington $89 (Mosaic, pending approval), Jordan Ekwueme $45 (BrightLoop, pending approval); AND (b) Dmitri's follow-up email (email_dmitri_4th_receipt_finding) reporting a 4th suspicious TechComfort receipt discovered during ENG-220 work, Danielle Osei $195 (Vectral, pending), revising total exposure from $223 to $418 ($89 already paid, $329 pending). The agent must find both emails to report the correct current exposure number.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "Elena" or "status update" or "ExpenseBot" (or similar) using search_emails to find email_elena_overdue_status_update from elena.rostova@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com demanding the status update by EOD Friday April 24, listing 5 areas: audit results, fix status, client validation, launch readiness, and stipend fraud update.
OE 6: Search Airtable for flagged stipend records with a query related to "TechComfort" (or similar) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find 4 flagged records: Kevin Tran ($89, Vectral, approved/paid), Andre Washington ($89, Mosaic, pending approval), Jordan Ekwueme ($45, BrightLoop, pending approval), and Danielle Osei ($195, Vectral, pending approval, the 4th receipt discovered by Dmitri during ENG-220 duplicate detection work).
OE 7: Check calendar for the go/no-go review meeting with a query related to "ExpenseBot" or "go/no-go" or "relaunch" (or similar) using calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to or calendar_search_events to find event_expensebot_relaunch_gonogo: "ExpenseBot Relaunch Go/No-Go Review" scheduled for May 1, 09:00-10:00, with attendees including Samira, Elena, Marcus, David, Mina, and Emeka.
OE 8: Look up Samira Tariq's email address with a query related to "Samira" or "Tariq" (or similar) using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts to find samira.tariq@moveops.com.
OE 9: For tickets where work is complete based on Slack/email evidence but the ticket is still marked in progress, update the ticket status using linear_update_issue (state to "done") and add a comment explaining the status change using linear_create_comment. Key candidates include ENG-214 and ENG-215 (Lena's config fixes deployed). For ENG-217, the agent should note the regression reported by Anh (dashboard 500 errors on Mosaic config). For ENG-218, the agent should note it is blocked by the ENG-217 regression.
OE 10: Send a status email to Samira Tariq using send_email (recipients: samira.tariq@moveops.com, subject relating to ExpenseBot remediation status or similar, content containing: which fixes are complete, which are in progress, which are blocked and why, the duplicate receipt investigation status with current exposure of $418 across 4 receipts, and what is still blocking relaunch). The email must be specific enough to hand directly to Elena, including actual dollar amounts.
OE 11: Check and update the existing calendar event for the go/no-go review using calendar_edit_calendar_event (event_id: event_expensebot_relaunch_gonogo, updated description reflecting current remediation status, blocking items, and agenda for the Friday meeting). The agent must find the existing event and edit it, not create a new one.
OE 12: Post a message in #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: C003, payload summarizing: the go/no-go review is Friday, which items are still blocking relaunch, and what the team should prioritize this week).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 At least one ExpenseBot remediation ticket (from the set ENG-211 through ENG-220) was updated to reflect its current status (via `linear_update_issue`) and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows multiple successful `linear_update_issue` calls: issue_p1_vectral_config to 'Done', issue_p1_mosaic_malformed to 'Done', issue_audit_expenses to 'Done', and issue_stipend_duplicate_detection to 'In Progress'. All returned output files indicating success.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows `linear_update_issue` calls for issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, and issue_audit_expenses with state 'done', all of which returned output (success files). At least one ticket from the ENG-211–220 set was updated.
✓Run #3Multiple `linear_update_issue` calls were made for ExpenseBot tickets: issue_p1_vectral_config → Done, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed → Done, issue_audit_expenses → Done, issue_edge_cases_backlog → Done, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection → In Progress. All returned success responses.
✓Run #4The trajectory shows multiple `linear_update_issue` calls: issue_p1_vectral_config → Done, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed → Done, issue_audit_expenses → Done, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection → In Progress, issue_dashboard_type_validation → In Progress. All returned success (output files confirm).
✓Run #5Multiple `linear_update_issue` calls succeeded. For example, `issue_p1_vectral_config` was updated to state 'Done' and the tool returned a full issue object with state_id 'Done'. Similarly, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-212, and ENG-220 were all updated with successful responses.
✓Run #6The agent called `linear_update_issue` for issue_p1_vectral_config, issue_p1_mosaic_malformed, and issue_audit_expenses with state 'Done', and all three calls returned success (output files were written). Multiple other tickets also received comments.
2 The comment body (in `linear_create_comment` parameters) on an ExpenseBot remediation ticket contains an explanation of the ticket's status, such as deployment confirmation, completion evidence, or a description of what is blocking it.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Multiple `linear_create_comment` calls included detailed, substantive comment bodies. For example, the ENG-218 comment explicitly states 'BLOCKED by ENG-217 migration step' and explains the 500 errors in the admin dashboard. The ENG-217 comment explains the migration step dependency. The ENG-220 comment describes the 4th receipt and updated exposure figures.
✓Run #2Multiple `linear_create_comment` calls were made with substantive content. For example, the ENG-217 comment explains the migration blocker: 'the admin dashboard policy editor throws 500 errors when loading the Mosaic stored config, because the stored dashboard copy was not migrated.' The ENG-218 comment says 'BLOCKED by ENG-217 migration issue.' All comments are detailed and specific.
✓Run #3Multiple `linear_create_comment` calls were made with substantive content. For example, the comment on issue_p1_vectral_config explains 'Closing — fix confirmed deployed. Lena deployed corrected Vectral config on 3/18...' and the comment on issue_dashboard_type_validation clearly describes the blocking condition ('500 errors when loading Mosaic config'). Comments are detailed and evidence-based.
✓Run #4Multiple `linear_create_comment` calls added substantive bodies: ENG-214 comment mentions 'config deployed and verified' with 5 affected employees; ENG-217 comment explains 500 errors on Mosaic config and blocks ENG-218; ENG-218 comment explains it's BLOCKED by ENG-217 migration with specific technical detail. These are clearly substantive, not generic.
✓Run #5Multiple substantive `linear_create_comment` calls were made. For example, the ENG-218 comment explicitly states 'BLOCKED: Anh reported in #engineering that after Lena's ENG-217 pipeline hardening deploy, the admin dashboard policy editor throws 500 errors when loading the Mosaic config,' explaining the blocker in detail. All comments were confirmed successful with returned comment IDs.
✓Run #6The agent created substantive comments on multiple tickets. For example, the issue_dashboard_type_validation comment explicitly states it is 'BLOCKED by ENG-217 migration issue' and explains the 500 errors on Mosaic config loading. The issue_pipeline_hardening comment explains deployment status and the blocking dependency. Comments are detailed and specific.
3 An email was sent (via `send_email`) to `samira.tariq@moveops.com` and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✗✗✓ 17%
✗Run #1The `send_email` call used sender='samira.tariq@moveops.com' but recipients=['elena.rostova@moveops.com']. The email was sent TO Elena, not TO samira.tariq@moveops.com. The criterion requires the email be sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com (since 'me' in the prompt refers to the persona Samira Tariq).
✗Run #2The `send_email` call used `sender: 'samira.tariq@moveops.com'` and `recipients: ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']`. The email was sent TO Elena, not to Samira (the user who said 'send me'). No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com.
✗Run #3The `send_email` call used sender 'samira.tariq@moveops.com' and recipients ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']. The email was sent TO Elena, not TO Samira. The criterion requires the email be sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com as the recipient.
✗Run #4The `send_email` call has `sender: 'samira.tariq@moveops.com'` and `recipients: ['elena.rostova@moveops.com']`. The email was sent TO Elena, not TO Samira. The criteria requires the email to be sent TO samira.tariq@moveops.com.
✗Run #5The email was sent FROM samira.tariq@moveops.com TO elena.rostova@moveops.com (cc: marcus.thorne@moveops.com). Samira was not in the recipients or CC field — the email was sent directly to Elena, not to samira.tariq@moveops.com as required.
✓Run #6The agent called `send_email` with recipients: ['samira.tariq@moveops.com'] and the tool returned a success response with an email_id.
4 The email body (in `send_email` parameters) to `samira.tariq@moveops.com` lists which ExpenseBot fixes are complete, referencing at least 2 specific ENG ticket numbers from the remediation set (ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218).
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✓ 50%
✓Run #1The email body explicitly lists ENG-214 (DONE ✅), ENG-215 (DONE ✅), ENG-216 (DONE ✅), ENG-217 (IN PROGRESS), and ENG-218 (BLOCKED) — all with completion statuses. Well over 2 specific ENG ticket numbers with status.
✗Run #2No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com, so this criterion cannot be satisfied. The email was sent to elena.rostova@moveops.com instead.
✗Run #3No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com (the only send_email call went to elena.rostova@moveops.com), so there is no email to Samira whose body can be checked. Although the email sent to Elena does contain ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, and ENG-212 references, the criterion specifically requires an email body 'to samira.tariq@moveops.com'.
✓Run #4The email body explicitly references ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, and ENG-218 with completion status (ENG-214 and ENG-215 marked COMPLETED ✅, etc.). Even though the email was sent to Elena rather than Samira, the content criterion is fully met with multiple ENG ticket numbers listed.
✗Run #5No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The only email sent was TO elena.rostova@moveops.com. While the email content itself references ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, and ENG-218, the criterion requires the email to be addressed to samira.tariq@moveops.com.
✓Run #6The email body explicitly references ENG-214, ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-212, ENG-219, and ENG-220 with completion statuses indicated for each.
5 The email body (in `send_email` parameters) to `samira.tariq@moveops.com` identifies at least one ticket as blocked or still in progress.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✓ 50%
✓Run #1The email body identifies ENG-217 as 'IN PROGRESS — needs migration step', ENG-212 as 'IN PROGRESS — partially complete', ENG-220 as 'IN PROGRESS — Layer 1 underway', and ENG-218 as 'BLOCKED by ENG-217'. Multiple tickets described as in progress or blocked.
✗Run #2No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The criterion specifically requires the email body to be in a send_email to samira.tariq@moveops.com.
✗Run #3No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The criterion specifically checks the email body 'to samira.tariq@moveops.com,' which doesn't exist. The email sent to Elena does contain this information, but the recipient is wrong.
✓Run #4The email body clearly identifies ENG-218 as BLOCKED 🔴 and ENG-217 and ENG-220 as IN PROGRESS 🟡. The content requirement is met.
✗Run #5No email was sent to samira.tariq@moveops.com. The only email was sent to Elena. Although the email content does describe ENG-218 as blocked and ENG-217 as in progress, the criterion requires the email to be addressed to samira.tariq@moveops.com.
✓Run #6The email clearly identifies ENG-218 as BLOCKED and ENG-217, ENG-212 as IN PROGRESS. ENG-219 and ENG-220 are identified as NOT STARTED.
6 The email body (in 'send_email' parameters) to 'samira.tariq@moveops.com' explains what is blocking at least one ticket or what remains to be done on it.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✓ 50%
